_about: Atelier project-level sandbox config. Allow list is intentionally tiny — only what the architecture needs. Deny list is the wall for --dangerously-skip-permissions (yolo mode).
_philosophy: Architecture is the wall, not the deny list. The host is supposed to be inert — every mutating op routes through bin/devbox into the VM. The allow list is the architectural surface; the deny list is the last-resort backstop for yolo mode. With --dangerously-skip-permissions, only the deny list still bites.
_deny_note: The sibling-projects guard (rm -rf $HOME/Code/crack/!(atelier)/**) assumes atelier is checked out at $HOME/Code/crack/atelier. If your layout differs, adjust this path to point at the parent of your projects so the guard actually protects them.
_write_scope: Write/Edit tools are NOT in the allow list, which means by default they prompt for any path. The project tree (./**) is auto-approved because the tool's defaults assume the project dir. To write elsewhere, the user must explicitly grant a path pattern.
